Keith et vous faites partie de Harvard Business Review Discussion Group

Infos

I am an experienced and result orientated Business Development Manager | Sales Manager | Account Manager with a proven track record of leading matrix style sales teams and winning new business through B2B direct sales and revenue generating partnerships for Fintech, SaaS and Digital Technology organisations. \\n\\nI am highly experienced at forming and leading cross functional / cross cultural teams through the full sales cycle and developing talent along the way. I bridge the gap between strategic plans and technical execution to deliver set goals, and I am confident building business relationships driven by curiosity to help understand customer pain points and deliver viable solutions. Furthermore, I schedule and organise work scopes and manage deal pipelines in CRM systems, including Salesforce and HubSpot. I am experienced identifying and qualifying new prospects through networking and outreach (cold calling) activities, and using grit / tenacity to overcome problems and achieve vision.\\n\\nDuring my career, I have worked for / with many top-tier Fintech & Banking organisation, including Temenos, Five Degrees, CGI, Salomon Smith Barney, Greenwich NatWest, ING Bank, ABN AMRO, Barclays, Bank Leumi & Vatican Bank. My achievements include:\\n\\n\uc0\u9989 A Tier 1 Dutch Bank wanted to deliver a new digital bank to its customers. As Business Development Manager, led cross functional team of pre-sales, marketing, product, legal & finance through sales process with the client, including initial conversations, RFP, POC, objection handling, commercial & legal negotiations, and successfully closed first new software licence for Temenos in the Netherlands in over 10 years with a \'80multi-million TCV.\\n\\n\u9989 A long-standing customer of Temenos grew dissatisfied with software and service provided. As Account Manager, engaged C-level stakeholders to understand frustrations and future strategy, assembled sales team, delivered series of workshops to demonstrate new capabilities of software & how Temenos would exceed requirements, and successfully recovered relationship, converting existing license to a new SaaS model.\\n\\n\u55349 \u56800 \u55349 \u56838 \u55349 \u56806 \u55349 \u56829 \u55349 \u56818 \u55349 \u56816 \u55349 \u56822 \u55349 \u56814 \u55349 \u56825 \u55349 \u56822 \u55349 \u56833 \u55349 \u56822 \u55349 \u56818 \u55349 \u56832 :\\n\\nSales Management | Fintech & Banking | Sales Strategy | B2B | New Business Development | Key Account Management | Lead Generation | Solution Selling | Competitive Analysis | Forecasting | Budgeting | CRM | Leadership | Team Building | Contract Management | Board Level Engagement | Inbound Sales & Marketing | RFI, RFP | Negotiation | Digital Transformation | Cloud Adoption & SaaS | Market Research | Strategic Partnerships | Analytics

  • Keith Morrison a republi\'e9 ce contenu

    FIS

    4 mois \'95

    Big news for FIS \'97 and for the future of financial services.

    We've completed our acquisition of Global Payments' Issuer Solutions business (formerly TSYS).

    This isn't just about scale.

    It's about what becomes possible when you bring banking, payments, and issuing together under one roof.

    This combination gives us more muscle to help banks stay ahead: better data, stronger infrastructure, and AI-powered tools that actually move the needle.

    Full details here:     http://spr.ly/6040CQFeC

    #FinancialServices #Payments #BankingInnovation #FinTech

        \'95 1er

        Fintech Solutions Specialist | Driving Growth in Financial Technology

        3 an(s) \'95

        I am very proud to see EPAM scoring so high in a very competitive landscape. Please reach out to me if you want to know more about EPAM and how we are achieving such impressive results.

        Whitelane Research

        3 an(s) \'95

        *2023 Dutch IT Sourcing Study Results Published*

        The 2023 Dutch IT Sourcing Study, conducted by Whitelane Research in collaboration with Eraneos - Powered by Quint, investigates more than 450 unique IT sourcing relationships and 740 cloud sourcing relationships held by over 220 participants of the top IT spending organisations in the Netherlands.

        This year, 24 IT service providers have been included in the general satisfaction ranking.         EPAM Systems achieves first place with 82%, followed by Tata Consultancy Services (81%) and Wipro (80%). The top three providers are classed as \'91Exceptional Performers\'92. Our report ranks the service providers by five IT service towers and four KPIs.

        To read more about the 2023 Dutch results, visit: https://lnkd.in/eXvHxKDe

        3 an(s) \'95 Modifi\'e9 \'95

        "We are pleased to share the results of another year of strong financial performance. Despite the challenges brought on by the war, our global team of professionals worked tirelessly to adapt while maintaining the trust of our customers and creating a more diversified global footprint," said Arkadiy Dobkin, CEO & President, #EPAM. "While the 2023 macro-environment remains uncertain, we are committed to accelerating our momentum while also ensuring that EPAM is well positioned for long-term success."

        2022 Performance Highlights:

        \u55357 \u56498 Revenues increased to $4.825 billion, a year-over-year increase of $1.067 billion, or 28.4%.

        \u55357 \u56425 \u8205 \u55357 \u56507 Total headcount was 59,300 as of December 31, 2022, an increase of 0.5% from December 31, 2021.

        \u11088 High growth areas include Travel & Consumer, Emerging, Life Sciences & Healthcare, Business Information & Media, Software & High-Tech.

        \u55357 \u56481 Review our full earnings report here: https://lnkd.in/e9N77Raa

        #EPAM #2022recap
